expositionText: My brother is dying. I haven't been able to get to him because the {enemy_type_plural} have been thick around here and I'm not the fighter I used to be. I need {kill_count} of them dealt with. I want to spend his last days at his side, not stuck in {settlement_name} listening to the wind.
acceptText: Thank you. I won't pretend this is easy to ask. Knowing you said yes makes the next part possible.
declineText: I understand. It's a heavy thing to put on a stranger. I'll find another way, or I won't, and either way I'll live with it.
expositionTurnInText: It's quieter now. I can hear myself think for the first time in days. There's something else I need before I can go to him, and I can't bring myself to face the looking.
conflict1Text: He always kept a {quest_item} on him. I'd like one in my hand when I sit down beside him: something familiar, something he'd recognize even now. If one turns up out there, bring it to me. I can't go searching myself.
conflict1TurnInText: That's the one. Or close enough that he'll know what I meant by it. I can feel my hands stop shaking just holding it.
conflict2Text: There's one more thing, and it's the hardest. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} should know. They were close to him once, before everything got complicated between them. Tell {target_npc_role} what's happening, in person. Don't soften it.
conflict2TurnInText: Then it's done. They know, and that's what mattered. I owed them that much, even if it cost something to send.
resolutionText: I'm going to him tonight. {quest_reward} is yours, and I wish I had more to give for what you carried with me. I won't forget that I didn't have to do this alone.

skillCheck.passText: I keep telling myself I'm ready. I'm not. I don't know who I am after this, and I'm afraid to find out. But sitting here pretending isn't the same as being there.
skillCheck.failText: It's a hard road. That's all there is to say.

rewardText: what coin I've set aside and a quiet thanks I won't take back

expositionText: I have to send my daughter away. There's nothing left for her in {settlement_name}, and the {enemy_type_plural} prowling out there make any road north a death sentence as it stands. I need {kill_count} of them gone before I can even think about putting her on a wagon. Every day I wait makes the choice harder, and I'm already barely holding to it.
acceptText: Then it's settled. I've been postponing this with every excuse I could find. Now I can't.
declineText: I see. I can't fault you, truly. Some weights aren't yours to lift, and I shouldn't have looked at you like they might be.
expositionTurnInText: Word came back that the way is clearer. I almost wish it weren't. There's still one more piece I can't manage from here, and it's the part I've been dreading most.
conflict1Text: I can't be the one to arrange it. {target_npc}, the {target_npc_role} in {target_npc_settlement}, agreed years ago to take her in if it ever came to this. I need you to go and tell {target_npc} the time has come. They'll understand what to prepare.
conflict1TurnInText: Then it's real. I've been pretending this day was theoretical for so long, and now it's just a date on a calendar. I'd ask you to leave me a moment if you didn't already know I needed it.
resolutionText: She leaves at the end of the week. I won't see her again for a long time, maybe ever, but she'll be safe and that has to be enough. Take {quest_reward}: it isn't much against what you helped me face, but I want you to have it.

skillCheck.passText: Can I tell you something I haven't said out loud? I'm not sure I'm strong enough to do this without flinching at the last moment. I keep thinking I'll find a reason to keep her here, and I know that reason would kill her eventually.
skillCheck.failText: It's a parent's burden. You'd understand it if it were yours.

rewardText: a small purse and a piece of bread for the road

expositionText: My wife asked me for one thing before she goes. A {quest_item}, the kind she used to keep when we were younger. I haven't been able to put one in her hand because I haven't been able to leave her side, and she doesn't have many days left. If you could find one for me, it would mean I kept my word at the end.
acceptText: Thank you. I'll tell her tonight that someone is looking. She'll smile, and that will be worth whatever this costs you to do.
declineText: I understand. It's an odd thing to ask of a stranger, and I shouldn't have laid it on you. I'll keep watching the door and hoping.
expositionTurnInText: She held it for a long time before she fell asleep. I haven't seen that look on her face in months. There's still more I need to manage before the end, and I can't do it from this chair.
conflict1Text: I'd hate to ask this of you too, but the {enemy_type_plural} have been bold enough that I can't even step outside to fetch the doctor. Take {kill_count} of them down. I need to be able to reach the road when the time comes, without leaving her alone for hours.
conflict1TurnInText: I went out for the first time in weeks and came back without my heart in my throat. That's a kindness I won't forget. There's one more thing, and it's the one I've been avoiding longest.
conflict2Text: Her brother {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. They haven't spoken in years, my fault as much as anyone's. He should hear it from a person, not a letter that arrives too late. Find {target_npc_role} and tell them plainly.
conflict2TurnInText: He's coming. I didn't think he would. Whatever you said to him, it found the right place to land. She'll see him before the end.
resolutionText: We have what time we have left, and now we can spend it without anything unsaid hanging over the room. {quest_reward} is yours, with my thanks. You gave us an ending I can live with.
rewardText: a wood-carved keepsake from my workbench and a few coins beside

expositionText: My husband passed last winter. I've made my peace with most of it, but there's a thing I can't quite let go of. He always wore a {quest_item}, and I lost the one he had on him in all the hurry afterward. If one turns up out there, even one that isn't his, I'd like to hold it again.
acceptText: Thank you. It sounds like such a small thing to ask for, but it isn't, and I think you already know that.
declineText: Of course. Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief. I'll be all right either way.
expositionTurnInText: Oh. I haven't held one of these since the morning before he left. It even smells the same, which I know it shouldn't, but it does. There's one more thing left undone, and I think I can finally face it now.
conflict1Text: His oldest friend {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. I sent word back when it happened, but a letter isn't the same as someone telling you. I'd like {target_npc_role} to hear how it really went, from a person who can answer questions. Would you bring the news?
conflict1TurnInText: Thank you. I should have done that myself, but I couldn't bear telling the story over and over to people who needed to ask. You carried that for me, and it was kind.
resolutionText: I think I'll sleep tonight. Properly, for the first time in a while. Take {quest_reward}: it isn't payment, it's a thank-you for something that doesn't have a price.

skillCheck.passText: What I miss most isn't the big things. It's how he hummed when he didn't know I was listening. I keep catching myself listening for it in {settlement_name} and feeling foolish.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a long winter. I'd rather not say more than that.

rewardText: a small ring of his that I have no use for now and won't see wasted

expositionText: I lost my son to {enemy_type_plural} a season ago. I'm not asking for revenge: I'm past that. But every time I see another one of them slinking around {settlement_name}, I feel like the world hasn't moved on, and I need it to. Take {kill_count} of them down for me. Not for him. For me.
acceptText: Thank you. I knew when I asked that this wouldn't bring him back. That isn't what I'm looking for anymore.
declineText: I understand. I shouldn't have made my grief into someone else's task. I'll keep trying to look the other way when I see them.
expositionTurnInText: It's a different quiet now. Not better, exactly, but different. I caught myself thinking about something other than him for a whole afternoon, and that felt like a small mercy. There's something else I'd like to ask, while I still have the courage.
conflict1Text: He always carried a {quest_item} when he went out. I never got the one he had with him back. I know it's silly, but if any one of them turns up in your travels, I'd like to put it on the shelf where his things sit. It doesn't have to be his. It just has to be the right kind.
conflict1TurnInText: That's enough. That's the one I'll keep. Looking at it doesn't hurt the way I thought it would, which is its own kind of strange.
conflict2Text: There's one more thread. His old hunting partner {target_npc} lives over in {target_npc_settlement}, and I never told {target_npc_role} how it happened. They deserved better than the rumor that reached them. Go and tell {target_npc} the truth, if you would.
conflict2TurnInText: Thank you. They needed to hear it, and I needed to know they did. That's the last thing I was holding onto.
resolutionText: I think I'm going to plant something this spring. I hadn't even thought about spring until today. {quest_reward} is yours, and I mean every bit of it: you helped me set down something I'd been carrying for too long.

skillCheck.passText: His grandfather was killed by {enemy_type_plural} too, before any of us were old enough to remember properly. I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now I wonder if I made him afraid of the wrong things.
skillCheck.failText: It's an old grief in this family. That's all I'll say tonight.

rewardText: a few of his things that should belong to someone who'll use them

expositionText: My mother passed two years ago this month. I've been meaning to mark the day properly and I keep putting it off. She used to carry a {quest_item} everywhere she went: said it brought her luck. I'd like to hold one again, just for the day. If one finds its way to you, bring it to me.
acceptText: Thank you. It's small. It matters more than it should, and I think you understand that.
declineText: That's all right. I've been carrying this on my own for a while. I can keep doing it.
expositionTurnInText: There it is. She would have laughed at me for getting sentimental over something so plain. I'm going to set it on the table tonight and have a meal with her, the way we used to. There's one other thing, if you're willing.
conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been close to {settlement_name} more often lately, and I can't sit with her memory in peace when I'm flinching at every sound outside. Take {kill_count} of them down. I want one quiet evening, that's all.
conflict1TurnInText: It's the first quiet night I've had in weeks. I lit a candle for her and just sat with it. Nothing grand, but it was what I needed.
resolutionText: I'll mark the day properly tomorrow. I haven't been able to do that in two years, and now I think I can. Take {quest_reward}: she would have wanted me to give something back to whoever helped.
rewardText: the silver clasp she left me, which is no use sitting in a drawer

expositionText: Something is wrong this season and I can't tell you what. The {enemy_type_plural} are showing up in numbers we haven't seen in years, in places they shouldn't even be. I'm not asking anyone to figure out why: I'm past that. I just need {kill_count} of them dealt with so we have a little room to breathe in {settlement_name}.
acceptText: All right. I won't pretend I think it'll fix the bigger thing. But it's something, and something is more than I had this morning.
declineText: I figured. Nobody knows what to make of any of this, and I can't blame you for not wanting to wade into it. We'll keep doing what we can on our own.
expositionTurnInText: That's a real difference. I almost forgot what it felt like to walk to the well without watching the treeline. But the strangeness hasn't lifted, and we're running short on what we need to keep going.
conflict1Text: We need {gather_count} {quest_item} just to get through the next stretch. I can't tell you why they've been so hard to come by lately, only that they have. Bring what you can. We'll stretch the rest.
conflict1TurnInText: That'll hold us. I don't understand why what used to take an afternoon now takes weeks, but I'm grateful you went out anyway. There's one more thing pressing on me, and I'd be a fool not to ask while you're here.
conflict2Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been creeping back in, the same ones I thought we'd cleared. I don't know if it's the same group or a new one, and I've stopped trying to make sense of it. Take down {kill_count} more. We just need another stretch of quiet.
conflict2TurnInText: Better. I won't say it's over, because I'm not sure anything is over, but it's better than it was. That counts for something tonight.
resolutionText: I don't understand half of what's been happening this season, and I've stopped expecting to. But we're still standing, and that's more than I'd have wagered a month ago. Take {quest_reward}: I want you to have it before the next strange thing finds us.

skillCheck.passText: You've seen this kind of pattern before? That's almost a relief. I'd started thinking I was the only one who noticed they move differently when the wind shifts. Maybe we're not as blind as I thought.
skillCheck.failText: It's all guesswork at this point. I'd rather just deal with what's in front of us.

rewardText: what we can spare from the storehouse and a warm meal besides

expositionText: Things have been off for weeks and I can't put a name to it. Crops are thinner, the well rope frayed twice in a month, and I can't get my hands on anything I need. We need {gather_count} {quest_item} or we won't make it to the next harvest in {settlement_name}. I'd go myself, but I'm one person against a whole lot of nothing-going-right.
acceptText: All right. Maybe with someone else trying, things will turn. They haven't yet, but maybe.
declineText: I expected as much, honestly. I'll keep at it on my own. That's been the shape of this whole season anyway.
expositionTurnInText: That's more than I've been able to gather in a fortnight. I don't know what's been blocking me when it comes so easy to you, but I've stopped trying to puzzle it. There's one more thing pressing, and it's gotten worse while you were out.
conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been showing up where they shouldn't, and I don't know if it's connected to the rest of it or just bad luck on top of bad luck. Take down {kill_count} of them so I can sleep without one ear open. That's all I'm asking for tonight.
conflict1TurnInText: I slept four hours straight. I haven't done that since this whole strange stretch began. Whatever's behind any of it, you've given me a piece of myself back.
resolutionText: I won't tell you we're past it: I don't think I'd believe me if I said it. But things look a little less crooked than they did. Take {quest_reward}, and know that an ordinary stretch of days is the most I'm hoping for now.

skillCheck.passText: You see it too, then. The way the air's been wrong in the early morning, like it isn't holding the heat it should. I'd thought I was imagining it. Knowing I'm not is almost worse.
skillCheck.failText: It's a strange season. Strange seasons happen. I'm trying not to dwell.

rewardText: a sack of what we have and the last good loaf out of my kitchen

expositionText: I've stopped trying to make sense of any of it. {enemy_type_plural} where there shouldn't be, tools breaking that have lasted decades, the well tasting wrong for a week. I need {kill_count} of those things cleared out as a start. Just so I can stop bracing every time I open the door in {settlement_name}.
acceptText: Thank you. I don't know if it'll help with the rest, but it'll help with this. I'll take what I can get this season.
declineText: Fair enough. I've gotten used to expecting nothing. It's easier than the alternative.
expositionTurnInText: That's a start. I won't pretend it's a solution, but the air feels less crowded. There's something else I've been chasing for weeks and getting nowhere with.
conflict1Text: There's a {quest_item} I've been trying to find since this stretch started. I can't tell you why I can never get my hands on one anymore: they used to be everywhere. If you find one, bring it to me. It would be the first thing this month that went the way I wanted.
conflict1TurnInText: There it is. I don't know whether to laugh or sit down. The smallest thing feels enormous when you've been failing at everything for so long.
conflict2Text: We need to lay in {gather_count} {quest_item} too, while we have the chance. The way things have been going, I don't trust that we'll be able to get them next week. I'd rather have too much and look foolish than have nothing and be right.
conflict2TurnInText: That's the most we've had on hand since this whole stretch began. I might actually breathe easy tonight, which would be a change worth marking.
conflict3Text: And one more. The {enemy_type_plural} are creeping back in already, the same as before, and I don't know if they ever really left. Take {kill_count} more down. I keep thinking each round will be the last one, and I keep being wrong.
conflict3TurnInText: Better. Quieter. I'm starting to wonder if sustained pushing back is the only answer when you can't see what you're pushing against. It's not understanding, but it's something.
resolutionText: I don't know what made any of this happen and I don't think I ever will. But we're standing, and the next stretch of days might even feel ordinary. Take {quest_reward}: I wasn't sure I'd ever have anything to give back, and it feels good to be wrong about that.

skillCheck.passText: Honest answer? Fighting something I can't name has worn me down more than the work itself. I keep waiting to wake up and have it all make sense, and it never does, and I don't know how much longer I can keep getting up.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a long stretch. I'd rather not turn it over right now.

rewardText: a fair share of what little we've managed to keep and my honest thanks
